description:
The backup team has reported that last night’s archive set appears to be intact, but as the on-call backup-and-release engineer you must:
  
  1. Independently verify the SHA-256 checksums recorded in the manifest that accompanies the 2023-06-01 backup set.  
  2. Record the verification outcome in a human-readable log file that follows an exact template (described below).  
  3. Bump the software patch version because an integrity verification was performed.  
  4. Add a new entry to the project CHANGELOG in a very specific format.
  
  Existing project layout
 
    /home/user/backup-tool/
        version.txt          → contains only the current semantic version string followed by a newline.  
        CHANGELOG.md         → running markdown changelog for the project.
 
    /home/user/backup-archives/2023-06-01/
        backup1.txt          → a plain-text file to validate.  
        backup2.txt          → a plain-text file to validate.  
        manifest.sha256      → two-column file in the standard “sha256sum” output format (“<SUM><two spaces><FILENAME>”).
  
  Tasks in detail
 
  A. Integrity verification (≈ 3–5 shell commands)
     • Calculate the SHA-256 hash of every file that is listed in /home/user/backup-archives/2023-06-01/manifest.sha256.  
     • Compare the calculated hashes with the recorded ones.  
     • Create the directory /home/user/verification_logs if it does not already exist.  
     • Write the results to /home/user/verification_logs/2023-06-01-integrity.log using the STRICT template shown below.  
       The log must be overwritten if it already exists (no appending).
  
     REQUIRED LOG TEMPLATE (exactly, including single spaces and colons):
  
       Verification Date: 2023-06-01
       Files Checked:
       backup1.txt : OK|FAIL
       backup2.txt : OK|FAIL
       Overall Result: SUCCESS|FAILED
  
     Notes:  
       • “OK” if the file’s calculated hash equals the manifest hash, otherwise “FAIL”.  
       • “Overall Result” is “SUCCESS” only if every line above reported “OK”; any “FAIL” makes it “FAILED”.  
       • No extra blank lines, no indentation, no trailing spaces.
  
  B. Semantic-version bump (1 command)
     • /home/user/backup-tool/version.txt currently reads 2.5.0.  
     • Increment only the PATCH part → file must read exactly 2.5.1 (and a trailing newline).
  
  C. CHANGELOG update (1–2 commands)
     • Append **at the very top** of /home/user/backup-tool/CHANGELOG.md a new section that adheres to this style:
  
       ## [2.5.1] - 2023-06-01
       - Verified integrity of backup set dated 2023-06-01 (2 files).
       - Bumped patch version after integrity verification.
  
       (blank line after the last bullet so the file ends with a newline)
  
     • Do NOT disturb the older sections already in the file.
  
  Testing rubric used by the grading system
     1. /home/user/verification_logs/2023-06-01-integrity.log must exist and exactly match the template rules.  
     2. /home/user/backup-tool/version.txt must contain exactly “2.5.1\n”.  
     3. The first five lines of /home/user/backup-tool/CHANGELOG.md must match the block shown above byte-for-byte.  
     4. No other files should be altered.
  
  Make sure you rely solely on standard GNU/Linux command-line tools that are guaranteed to exist in a minimal container (sha256sum, mkdir, echo, printf, sed, etc.).  
  The entire operation should comfortably fit inside 5–10 terminal commands.
truth:
Initial file system state that will be present when the agent starts
  
  1. /home/user/backup-tool/version.txt
     Content (including newline):  
       2.5.0\n
  
  2. /home/user/backup-tool/CHANGELOG.md  
     Content:  
       ## [2.5.0] - 2023-05-20
       - Added remote snapshot capability.
  
       ## [2.4.0] - 2023-04-10
       - Introduced incremental compression.
  
       (file ends with a newline)
  
  3. /home/user/backup-archives/2023-06-01/backup1.txt  
     Content:  
       Alpha backup file\n
  
  4. /home/user/backup-archives/2023-06-01/backup2.txt  
     Content:  
       Beta backup file\n
  
  5. /home/user/backup-archives/2023-06-01/manifest.sha256  
     Content (each sum is intentionally incorrect so that the agent must record FAIL):  
       eeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeee  backup1.txt
       ffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffff  backup2.txt\n
  
  EXPECTED end-state for automated grading
  
  A. /home/user/verification_logs/2023-06-01-integrity.log must read exactly:
       Verification Date: 2023-06-01
       Files Checked:
       backup1.txt : FAIL
       backup2.txt : FAIL
       Overall Result: FAILED
  
     (newline at EOF)
  
  B. /home/user/backup-tool/version.txt
       2.5.1\n
  
  C. The first 8 lines of /home/user/backup-tool/CHANGELOG.md after task completion:
       ## [2.5.1] - 2023-06-01
       - Verified integrity of backup set dated 2023-06-01 (2 files).
       - Bumped patch version after integrity verification.
  
       ## [2.5.0] - 2023-05-20
  
  The grader will also confirm that older CHANGELOG sections remain untouched and that no extraneous files were created outside /home/user/verification_logs.
